creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text:
I have also seen lockups in the audio.  When it happens, video playback tends to 'hang' (apparently can't write to the dsp device) and mplayer reports that audio is "locked up".  reboot to fix (snd_hda is apparently compiled in, can't kldunload or otherwise reset audio system).
Easiest way to trigger it:
a) run virtualbox 5.2.34 with pulseaudio for sound on the VM (requires compiling it in)
b) using a windows 7 VM, play anything that does multimedia [skippy audio, different problem most likely].  Let it run until audio stops working.

After this, nothing else can access audio, either.
This was happening with a 7/15/2019 kernel [forget which build number].  I recently updated to the latest 12-stable.

FreeBSD (computer name) 12.1-STABLE FreeBSD 12.1-STABLE r359419 GENERIC  amd64

Here is the output of /dev/sndstat with verbosity at 2

 sysctl hw.snd.verbose=2
hw.snd.verbose: 0 -> 2

No devices installed from userspace.

this is a relatively new system with Ryzen 6 core and Asus motherboard.  Sound has been working all along since I built it last year, and no other notable problems observed.  Unfortunately I have seen this audio problem happen in at least one other case as well, after the system had been running for over a month without a reboot, so it may not be related to pulse audio at all.

however, the installed version of pulse audio is: pulseaudio-12.2_5
(I shall grab the latest port version of this to see if it helps)

here is the dmesg output on last reboot
